reasoning: All three locations read $_SERVER['HTTP_HOST'] but the attacker-controlled value is never the output domain injected into URLs or redirects. (1) fix_cross_domain_assets() (main file line 142): HTTP_HOST is used only as a lookup key against a DB-stored whitelist of Polylang domains (pll_options['domains']); substitution only fires if the spoofed host exactly matches a trusted DB entry, making the attacker unable to inject a foreign domain. (2) ConnectPlugins::fix_url_domain() (connect-plugins.php line 855): HTTP_HOST is a fallback only when pll_current_language('home_url') is falsy; even when reached, the spoofed value is the str_replace() needle (source to remove), while the replacement is always the DB-stored pll_get_post_language(..., 'home_url'). The output URL domain is never attacker-controlled. (3) ElementorAssets::editor_domain_redirect() (elementor-assets.php line 182): HTTP_HOST is again the needle for str_replace(); the redirect target is the DB-stored $post_host from pll_get_post_language(), and wp_safe_redirect() further restricts destinations to allowed hosts. Additionally this hook runs only on admin_init (authenticated users) and only in Polylang multidomain configurations. No path exists where a spoofed Host header causes an attacker-controlled domain to appear in script/style URLs or Location headers.
